Introduction to HTML Testing

This is a sample document for testing the HTML extractor. It contains various elements that should be properly extracted with hierarchy preservation.

Basic Structure

This paragraph demonstrates basic text extraction from HTML. It should be captured as a single chunk with proper hierarchy tracking showing the h1 and h2 headings.

Subsection Example

Here we have a level 3 heading with some content beneath it. The hierarchy should show all three levels properly nested.

Lists and Elements

HTML supports various element types that contain text:

  • First list item with some text content
  • Second list item demonstrating list extraction
  • Third list item with additional details

This is a blockquote element that should be extracted as a separate chunk with appropriate metadata.

Divisions and Paragraphs

Content inside div elements should also be extracted properly. This paragraph is nested within a div.

References Section

This section contains sample references. See Chapter 5 for more details. Compare with Section 2.3 mentioned above.

Biblical Citations

This text mentions Romans 8:28 and Psalm 23:1-6 as sample scripture references that should be detected by the extraction system.

Conclusion

This final section wraps up the test document. It should be extracted with proper metadata including the title from the title tag and author from meta tags.
